channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel nominated as Ohio's lieutenant governor
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine on Monday nominated former Ohio State Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel to be the state's next lieutenant governor. Tressel retired a year and a half ago as president of Youngstown State University, a job he had held since 2014.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kendrick Lamar and SZA are expanding their tour to Europe
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fresh off their Super Bowl halftime spectacle, Kendrick Lamar and SZA are expanding their upcoming tour to make stops in Europe. The additional stops announced Monday include a July 4 show in Frankfurt, Germany.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Third judge blocks Trump’s order ending birthright citizenship for kids of people in US illegally
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A third federal judge on Monday blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S. illegally. The ruling from U.S. District Judge Joseph N.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Luigi Mangione accepts $300k from 'sick' donors cheering murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO: expert
item.description: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione has accepted nearly $300,000 in donations for his criminal defense from the December 4 Legal Committee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state Democrats spearhead bills to crack down on campus antisemitism
item.description: More than a year after the Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack on Israel sparked U.S. campus protests, New York lawmakers propose a crackdown on hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl halftime performer detained after unfurling Sudanese-Palestinian flag
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A performer in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show was detained on the field and could face charges after unfurling a combination Sudanese-Palestinian flag with “Sudan” and “Gaza” written on it. The NFL confirmed the person was part of the 400-member field cast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Super Bowl LIX highlights: Memorable plays, moving tributes and a historic presidential visit
item.description: The Philadelphia Eagles defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 40-22 in the first Super Bowl to be held in New Orleans in a dozen years. It was played amid heavy security — and with President Donald Trump in attendance — just 41 days after a man plowed a truck into New Year's revelers on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uh oh 😖 Texas among least educated states in America, study says
item.description: Yikes! It looks like Texas needs to hit the books after a recent study named the Lone Star State among the least educated states in the country.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Humble, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KREX 5

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets the NFL's season rushing record, including the playoffs
item.description: Saquon Barkley set an NFL single-season rushing record in the Super Bowl, held his young daughter as a torrent of confetti fluttered around them and flashed a smile as he held a Philadelphia newspaper with “CHAMPS!” stamped above his photo.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutor says Salman Rushdie was too stunned to react when a man started to stab him
item.description: A prosecutor says Salman Rushdie was so stunned when a masked man started to stab him on a stage in western New York that the acclaimed author didn’t even try to fight back.
item.dc:creator: Carolyn Thompson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: The meaning behind Kendrick Lamar's jacket during the Super Bowl halftime show
item.description: Kendrick Lamar sported a letterman-style jacket during his Super Bowl halftime performance Sunday, but it wasn't repping either team on the field.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UPDATE: Coroner IDs 7-year-old, adult killed in Urbana home invasion
item.description: Update 5 p.m. URBANA, Ill. (WCIA) -- The Champaign County Coroner has identified the child and adult that died in a Saturday night shooting in Urbana.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Morando, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Drivers targeted in emerging crime trend have one thing in common
item.description: Texas law enforcement issued a word of warning to Ford truck owners after they arrested a trio of thieves who targeted the high-end truck model by stealing taillights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people, while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anthony Davis leaves his Dallas debut with an injury after seismic Luka Doncic trade
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Anthony Davis left in the third quarter with a lower-body injury and didn't return in his debut with the Dallas Mavericks on Saturday after the seismic trade that sent Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nelly delivers hits at 'Homecoming' Super Bowl week concert in historic New Orleans restaurant
item.description: Nelly brought his signature St. Louis swagger to a New Orleans institution, turning a nearly seven-decade-old restaurant into a Super Bowl week party.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, ABC 4

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Missing commuter plane found crashed on Alaska sea ice and all 10 aboard are dead, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — A small commuter plane that crashed in western Alaska on its way to the hub community of Nome was located Friday on sea ice, and all 10 people on board were dead, authorities said. The crash was one of the deadliest in the state in the last 25 years.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NASCAR honors 'The Ironman' Ricky Rudd and 'Cousin Carl' Edwards as Hall of Fame inductees
item.description: One of the toughest drivers in NASCAR history joined one the most enigmatic drivers of the modern era as the newest inductees into the Hall of Fame in a star-studded Friday night ceremony.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: States absorb big increases in Medicaid for sicker-than-expected enrollees after COVID-19 pandemic
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — States are absorbing substantial increases in health care costs for the poor, as they realize that the people remaining on Medicaid rolls after the COVID-19 pandemic are sicker than anticipated — and costlier to care for.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Trump is trying to halt the EV charger buildout. Experts say it's not that easy
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Experts are questioning President Donald Trump’s latest effort to slow the electric vehicle charging buildout in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, WGN 9

item.title: San Jose State and Penn face inquiries in Trump crackdown on transgender athletes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is investigating potential civil rights violations at two universities and a high school sports league that allowed transgender athletes to compete on women's teams, the Education Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4
